passages.text: Social media has changed the way we communicate. Fifteen years ago, people mainly used phones and emails to stay in touch. Today, billions of people use platforms like Instagram and TikTok every day. While social media helps us connect with friends far away, it also has disadvantages. Many young people feel pressure to look perfect online. If you spend too much time on social media, you might start comparing yourself to others. Experts recommend limiting screen time to two hours per day. I have reduced my own usage recently, and I feel much happier.

questions.question: How did people mainly communicate fifteen years ago?
What problem does social media cause for young people?
options: They feel pressure to look perfect

questions.question: What do experts recommend for screen time?

passages.title: Social media has changed the way we communicate

passages.text: I have been studying English for five years, and I can now hold a conversation quite well. However, I still make mistakes when I speak. My teacher says that making mistakes is part of the learning process. If you are afraid of making mistakes, you will never improve. She also told me that I should watch films in English and read books to expand my vocabulary. I have already watched ten films this month. I think my listening has improved a lot. My next goal is to write a short story in English.

questions.question: How long has the writer been studying English?
What does the teacher say about mistakes?
What is the writer's next goal?

passages.text: Last weekend, my friends and I decided to go camping. We had planned the trip for months. We packed our tents, sleeping bags and food, and drove to a lake in the mountains. When we arrived, it started raining heavily. We could not start a fire, so we ate cold sandwiches for dinner. Despite the bad weather, we had a great time telling stories and playing cards. The next morning, the sun came out and we went swimming in the lake. If the weather had been better, we would have stayed an extra day. I think we will try again next month.

questions.question: What did the writer and friends do when it started raining?
What did they do the next morning?
What would the group have done with better weather?

passages.title: Last weekend, my friends and I decided to go camping
